sentences.text: Hello!
Hello!
Hello, how are you?
Good!
Hey, Bobby.
How's your morning going so far?
Been pretty busy, it's been a busy week.
How about y'all's?
also the same.
We had a full company off-site this week in Chicago, so the whole company got together in Chicago for 3 days.
Oh, nice.
How was the weather?
Was it….
Was it pretty nice?
Temperature-wise, it was nice, but it downpoured.
We had, like, a outdoor beach event on Monday night, and it was, like, torrential downpouring.
Oh, no.
Yeah, it actually made it kind of fun, but….
Core Memory, core memory.
picture.
And where were y'all based out of?
The two of us are in Madison, Wisconsin.
Oh, okay, nice.
No, well, it's a cool town.
It is, you've been?
I have, yeah, I actually went to college in, Twin Cities.
Nice.
Oh.
So, spent a little time up in that area.
Yeah.
Where do you live now?
Panama City Beach, Florida.
Hold on.
What's up?
Where are you from originally?
I've lived, honestly, everywhere.
I'm a bit of a nomad.
I grew up in Philadelphia.
I've lived everywhere from… Tampa, New York City, Miami, LA, Scottsdale, Las Vegas, Denver… I was in Austin, Texas for a long time, and then, just moved here from Memphis.
I've lived everywhere.
Yeah.
What brought you….
Vegas.
What brought you to Panama City Beach?
I used to, spring break there as a kid.
Oh, yeah?
So my family is down here, and my wife's family's, … Moving to the area as well.
So, just trying to get a little bit closer to family.
Yeah, love that.
Yeah.
Hey, Noel!
How are you?
Good, how are you?
Doing well.
I just pinged the rest of the team.
We're a Teams household, so no one ever gets a notification when we have a Zoom, so I'm sure they'll jump on in a minute.
All good.
We are a Zooms, so when Kylie or I can on Teams, we become We also don't know what to do.
So, I had one this morning, and it's like, when I share my screen, I can't see the people I'm talking to.
Yeah.
you know, nods or signs that they're still there.
That's tough.
You gotta get used to it, for sure.
Yeah.
Hey, Steven!
Hello, Kylie.
Hello, Patrick.
Steven, what's going on?
Good, good.
Sorry, I did not, as Noelle pointed out in chat, I did not get pinged.
I was late.
Is there a way that you could update the calendar invites so we actually get notified?
Did you get notified, Noel?
I don't know, honestly, I ignore my notifications for calls, because I have somebody on my calendar that I don't always have to attend.
like….
I'm not sure.
I don't know if it's a me problem, or….
Sounds like it's a problem.
Bobby said he got pinged.
Yeah, it looks like it's set for 10 minutes before.
… But I can look and see why you might not be getting it.
Weird.
Is Outlook your email provider, or is it Gmail?
Outlook.
Oh, yeah, I don't know, maybe that's it.
I don't know, like, I didn't… I promised I would join on time, but… It's okay.
So again, no worries.
Well, thank you guys for joining.
I was thinking for the call today.
I know that Bobby is new to this, so I thought that it might make sense to do, like, a high-level overview and kind of go through, like, a high-level demo.
Wanted to kind of go over, like, the what we heard, for the first couple conversations that we had with this team of why you guys decided to roll it out, so that Bobby's aware.
And then go over a couple, new features, product updates, and then just share pricing.
Is there anything else that you guys wanted to chat about?
Anything you wanted to add into this while we're on?
Sounds good.
I see your team's growing a lot, Steve.
Oh, sorry, go ahead, Steven.
So I'm gonna say, I… would… For these calls, I would eventually like to see, like, organic lift.
and some other of those metrics.
I presume we will start getting organic lifts through G, Google Business Profile, but… It'd be good to see, like, a before and after and share that with everyone.
Okay, yeah, we can definitely do that.

We… I would say this is an organic traffic tool, so it does take a little bit of time to see, like, really strong lift, and I know we've been slowly rolling out for the last couple months, so I can definitely pull what we're seeing so far, and send that over to you.
How's your face?
Steven, it looks like the team's growing.
I keep seeing on LinkedIn people are being added.
Is that….
Smart team, it's Bobby.
So, everyone else is not really on the marketing side.
Okay.
I saw some, folks that are joining Pegasus, so it looks like some good things are happening.
Over there.
Bobby, what was your… when was your first day?
I think it was, like, 3 weeks ago.
Three weeks ago, so… He's still, figuring out how we, how we do things, but he is very knowledgeable in marketing.
Nice.
Congrats, man.
Welcome to the team, Bobby.
I appreciate it.
No, and I, I appreciate the, the overview of, Maven, and this will be my first exposure to it, so, love to hear all the slick features you guys got, and how you're gonna make us successful.
Awesome.
Awesome.
And it'll be the three of you splitting the portfolio, is that right?
Correct.
Okay, cool.
So, Noelle, I know that you oversee more of, like, the Lisa properties.
So that's changed a bit since Bobby came on, so we kind of split the portfolio even now, so we all have a little bit of a mix.
Nice, awesome.
Cool.

Well, typically, these check-in calls are a little bit more, like, exactly what Steven's asking for, looking at performance, where we're at with performance, partnership overview, so we'll go over, like, the amount of properties we have on, and talk a little bit more, like, strategically with initiatives you're all focusing on.
And we could definitely do that today, too, I just know that with Bobby starting, I wanted to do a little bit more of, like, a traditional demo, so….
Go for it.

Well, well, starting with, back to, like, the original conversations that we had and why we're here, why you guys have decided to partner with us, is there was a need, or kind of like a want to expand the features from previous tools you were working with for Google Business Automation.
And that there were some properties that were doing manual posting, so creating content and then manually posting to the Google profiles.
Obviously, that's time-consuming, and that an automated tool would be helpful for, team efficiency, but then also consistency profile to profile.
And the products feed, I believe was the feature that was most exciting for this team.
So, getting live pricing and availability fed over directly from your feed to your Google profiles.
Anything I'm missing there, Steven?
For reasons why you guys had signed on.
It's part of our geo strategy, I would add to that, so… By geo, as in, like, reviews, content, local SEO, like, it's going to be pulling from that.
Google AI, … that Google AI overview bot, that's going to be pulling from that, so… It's part of that strategy, and it's very hands-off.
That's why we ultimately like Maven.
Yep.
Yep.
We've been hearing that exact thing from a lot of our other customers with.
the focus on Q&A, and a lot of customers have been starting to roll out FAQs on their websites, because of that exact reason, that the AI overviews like that type of content.
So that's the exact same thing, just getting the Q&A on your Google profiles.
So, definitely something that we're hearing a lot of.
So, the overview, Bobby, for you is that this… we have our Google Business Automation tool.
You are… have also rolled out our social posting tool, so we'll cover that, but that's automated posting to social profiles, as well as our listings management tool.
So, Pegasus has opted into those 3 products of Maven.
Got it.
Cool, well, I'll jump into what the product looks like.
So, grab some screenshots from your properties.
This is the products feed.
So, the first feature, and the one that was most interesting, from what we heard from you guys, was getting this live pricing and availability onto your profiles.
So, everything that we go over today is all fed from your property management softwares.
So that's, like, the source of truth for all the information that we're pulling over.
And the products feed is updating, with Rent Cafe, it's 4 times a day, pinging the feed to pull what you have available at the floor plan level.
With the lowest price per floor plan as the pricing.
Any questions on that?
It's a long time.
Pretty straightforward.
No.
And then, the next piece is, also lives in the products feed.
This is a newer feature that we've rolled out, but this is amenities that live within the products feed.
So here you can see we're pulling in community-level amenities, unit-level amenities, all directly from the feed to your profiles.
Little to… I mean, no work that needs to be done on your end.
We're just pulling in the amenity fields, and then our tool matches a photo to that amenity.
I think when talking to clients, if you ever… interface with clients, of the, like, the ownership groups.

Like, this… is a great way to get every aspect of their property that lives on the website, that lives on their ILSs, now directly onto Google without having for your team, any manual effort to do so.
So, Steven is absolutely right, like, Google… creating and putting more content on Google gives you a better shot to be aggregated in the AI overview, so that's a benefit here, too.
But also, visually, and for local search, now every aspect of your property is delivered onto Google automatically.
And then, sorry, I'm still trying to… get a lay of the land, but, like, for our one, property, Noel, with the workspaces terminal.
Can, can we, highlight work Like, rentable workspaces in this area, as well?
Is that an amenity that you would have at the building?
At the property?
I'm sorry, say that again?
Is that an amenity that would be at the property?
Like, workspaces that you can rent?
Or is it, like, an actual co-working space?
They're, they're actual working spaces that both residents and… Non-residents can, can rent out.
Let me look up that property.
What's it called?
And I can do work in the background while we do it.
Union Terminal Warehouse?
Yeah, Terminal Warehouse.
So, to give you a little bit of background on that deal, it is part apartments, part, … Industrial slash office, because there's, like, a light industrial… area within there.

And it's like a refurbished… Like, warehouse sort of deal, where they were able to add apartments, and some of them, they kept it as… like this… Like, expect, like, exposed concrete floors, ceilings… That, like, industrial loft style.
Bobby, are you requesting to just have it listed as an amenity, or do you mean, like, the availability and, like, actually having it listed out, like, in the floor plans?
I guess I just want to know what capabilities we do have.

I mean, it'd be great if we could, A, highlight the workspaces, and then B, if there… if there was a way that it, like, there was a contact form, and we can push leads into NOC, … Just trying to think through some outside-the-box ideas.
Let me… let's get back to you on that one.
So, where is that?
Is that in New York?
Jacksonville?
Jacksonville, the terminal.
Terminal warehouse.
Couldn't find it on Google.
It's a Union Terminal Warehouse, if that's helpful.
Union Terminal….
It is a Google business profile listed as an apartment complex?
Apartment building.
There it is.
It is, yeah.
So then the… the workhouse side of it is… it's a whole separate entity, so that's called Workhouse at Union Terminal.
So they have their own Google My Business, own website, own everything.
Yeah.
I think we could… Yeah, we'd have to optimize the union, like, the apartment Google business profile.
Versus the industrial, like, the workspace one.
But… If you have the workspace listed as an amenity, we could put it here.
what Kylie's showing, but we couldn't… we aren't able to sync, like.
guest cards over to the workspace, I don't think.
Like, this is a multi-family specific product, so I don't think we could really help on the workspace profile.
Yeah, but if you had the… Rentable working spaces as an amenity in the field, then we'd be able to pull it over that way.
Got it.
It would just be listed on the apartment complex Google Business profile as an amenity.
Got it.
Okay, that's helpful.
Thank you.
Yep.
The next feature of this is daily posts.
So we know that Google prioritizes profiles that are updating frequently, so we are posting a unique post every day.
So just like everything else, the… we're pulling the amenity-based information and the photos directly from your feed, and then our AI tool is creating this content, so it's unique every day.
Also, a trigger that we know Google looks at is uniqueness.
So, unique content every day.
Every day, the post will have a photo, and the content will match that photo.
So, a bit more of, like, an organic SEO play for the posts, but also a great way to fill your profile out and make sure it's fully optimized.
A couple of features within the posts.
One is concession posts, so pulling concession data directly from your feed to your profile.
We know a lot of time and energy and thought goes into creating these concessions, so this is just another way to get them visible in one more place.
So it's… you'll see one post per week, as long as your property's running a concession, will be a concession highlighting that concession.

These posts also fall off or get removed as soon as that concession is removed from the feed or the expiration date is hit, so we're not storing any outdated or old concessions on the profile, this is more of, like, a live post.
Great way to automate a manual process.
Like, a lot of our customers, if they did manually post, they would do so because they had a concession.
This is a way to completely take that off.
Either your plate or on-site team member that maybe had to do it.
Clients love it, it's accurate, it's fresh.
And you don't have to worry about remembering to post it on Google.
Oh, very cool.
Like it.
One other feature of the posts, is that we can insert SEO keywords throughout the posts.
So, just so you know, we can intake a list of SEO keywords that you either want to rank higher on, or are already ranking high on, and insert them throughout the posts.

So a lot of customers are using that for highlighting, like, the neighborhood they're in, or that they're near a park or a university, things that we wouldn't be grabbing from the feed, that you want to get entered throughout the posts.
So that would be one part, like, really the only part that is manual is we would need a list of those SEO keywords.
But just so you're aware, this is a feature that's included with this as well.
Any questions on the Google side before I hop into the social posting?
Not for me.
Alright.
Well, you guys have also opted in to our automated social posting.

So, similar concept to the posts that we're doing on Google, where it's… a lot of the content is property-specific, ways of highlighting amenities, in the unit, amenities at the property, and then we've actually introduced a few new post types that I wanted to show all of you.
But what we're doing here is posting directly from the feed as well.
So, that's where we're getting all of this content directly from the feed.
Different content than what we're posting to Google.
So, you'll see the posts on Google that are different from the posts that we're doing on your social pages.
And here is an overview of what post types we're doing.
So, like I said, a lot of it's property-specific content, but then we've also had customers ask us to, diversify the content a little bit, in terms of we've been adding holiday posts.
Scheduled tour posts, pet policy posts.
So we've added in some additional post types.
For this tool.
Completely automated.

We've had customers that roll this out, so that they get consistent property-specific content every single month, and then they also have maybe, like, a local team that does reels, or… TikTok, or whatever, you know?
So, this is just a way to make sure you have consistent content at all times across your portfolios, profiles.
That makes sense.
And then, do you have reporting?
Obviously, you have reporting as far as… You know, which campaigns get the most traction, and … Impressions.
So we have reporting on the Google side that shows change in traffic over time, so change in map impressions, change in clicks to the website.
We do not have a reporting tool on the social side, because these are really just posts that are going onto your profiles.
Got it.
It's not… it's not paid ads, it's organic content.
I think eventually we'll have some reporting around impressions of the posts, but for now, it's the automation that most of our customers roll this out for.
Makes sense.
We also have a full dashboard, for both products that I wanted to share.
Steven, Noel, have either of you logged into the dashboard?
Have you been, like, reviewing posts that are upcoming?
Not yet.
Okay, yeah, and that… that's not uncommon.

A lot… I mean, most customers roll us out for the automated purpose, so a lot of our customers aren't even going in and reviewing the post because they trust the… the AI, and they like the posts that they're seeing, but there is always the ability to go into a dashboard to see all the upcoming posts.
So in here, you can click which property you want to see, click all properties to review content, and then you can also sort by channel.
So if I wanted to see all properties, all channels, I can click in here and see all upcoming posts.
So, I do have a question.
Do we have any flexibility on when we want to schedule those posts, or does the AI take into consideration, certain times and days of the week?
In which they, you know, that will get the most out of that post.
So, for Google, we're posting every day, and then for social, we're posting for you guys 3 times a week.
So, 3 times a week, it is… Oh, I wish… Tuesday… Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday, I think, are the three.
I can verify the exact days that they're going out, but, we've set it up daily for Google, and then three times a week for social.
Okay.
So there wouldn't be, like, a situation where… it just fires off, like, two posts right after each other, like, you know, one at 12, and then one at 12.
It's pretty spread out, right?
Yep.
Yeah, it's pretty spread out, and we did the 3 times a week for social based on what customers said they were asking their teams to do.
So when their teams were manually doing this, what were they… what were they holding them accountable for?
And most customers said 3 times.
There is flexibility there if you… Wanted to look at something else, or if you had a property that just didn't have enough content for 3 a week, we can customize it, too.
Sounds good.
Thank you.
Yeah.
So I wanted to show this dash, because in here, you can not only see the upcoming post that the tool has created, but you can also edit if you ever wanted to.
So if you wanted to edit the content, or if you wanted to swap a photo out, you can do that in here.
If you wanted to just fully deny a post, you can do that here, and then our tool will regenerate a post in its place for that same day.
And then we've recently rolled out the ability to also manually create posts.
So we had some customers saying that it's helpful to see all of the automated posts, but then their team is still going into Meta to create posts.
So this gives you the ability to manually create posts, And right in this dash.
So here you can insert your content, and then drag and drop photos or videos.
You can post now, or you can schedule these out.
So, any questions on that?
Nope.
Noelle, Steven, have you… I don't think maybe this button is new for you guys, this is a new feature for us, this manual content creation.
Is this something that you guys could see yourself utilizing?
Or have interest in using?
I definitely like that it's all in one dashboard.
Bobby, you were talking about Union Terminal Warehouse earlier, and I'm sure you remember from the call.
So, transparently, they use Social Capture for their organic, social posting, but they're just not super happy.

with the content being pushed out, so Bobby, this might be something that we want to look into, because they do want to be able to see, like, a content calendar kind of like this, to where they can look, and then… because it's obviously supplemental to the on-site teams.

Because they, you know, we're really pushing them to do more, like, localized, more apartment-specific that obviously, like, a bot or, you know, any team that's doing third-party posting for them won't know.
But yeah, Bob, you might want to look into this for the apartment side, specifically.
Additionally, because I know I think they have a ticket in right now, a tailor, about, having one dashboard to post everything, and so this would kind of knock out two birds with one stone.
Just might be something.
No, no, that's a great suggestion.
I was… you and I are on the same page.
I think there may be some value here for them.

Yeah, well, this is included, in the social tool that you guys have been rolling out with us, so, just another feature I wanted to make sure you guys knew, because we… we had some customers saying we… they're choosing us for the automation, and then they were still hanging on to, like, scheduling tools for this reason.

So this is… this is just another reason, like, you can come in here, I can see, okay, in the next 3 days, here's the posts that are coming, this is the day I want to add my, community event, or highlighting pet of the month, or whatever.
So, yeah, a great way to do it all in one dash.
Yeah, that's super helpful, especially for, you know, the push that we want to make in 2026.
this will help justify it as an addition, especially for properties that are currently using Sochi, which we… is kind of like a lesser evil.
We don't love Sochi, but we like that it gives the teams access.
But if we can just get them on this, and then it'll do automatic posting for them, as well as they can use it as a scheduler tool as well, I mean, that's a no-brainer.
Yep, yeah.

Yeah, and it's included in what you guys have been signing up for, so… To wrap this up, Bobby, for you, the pricing that we've come up with with the Pegasus team is $140 per property, and that includes everything that we went over, including listings management.
I know we didn't really dive into it.

It's the dashboard that pushes your content, or your, … your property-specific, like, hours of operations, phone numbers, all of that, like, local listing information from, like, Google and Yelp and Bing, that's included in this as well.
So, this price is all-encompassing for the Google tool, the social tool, and listings management.
Very cool.
I like it.
Cool.
Anything else you guys wanted to chat through today?
I think so.
Not me.
Kylie, I'll probably reach out to you about that Union Terminal, property after we have some internal discussions.
I'm meeting with the owners at some point, I think, next week, and I want to understand, some of their pain points, but….
To Noah's point, I think this might be a good solution for them.
Yeah.
Awesome.
Let us know.
If there's any questions that come up in that call, let me know.
If there's any materials I can provide prior to the call, let me know.
I'll send over this deck so you have it.
I'll also send over… we have a one-sheeter that kind of wraps all of this up into one.
I can send you that as well.
That's great, yeah, thank you.
Yeah.
And then, Steven, I'll get you, I'll pull a reporting, and get over what we're seeing so far.
But if there's anything else that we can provide, let me know.
Awesome.
I appreciate your time today, Kylie.
Of course.
Nice meeting you.
Welcome to… Great to be working with you.
Alright, well, y'all have a good weekend.
Thank you, you too.
Thanks.
Yeah, bye.
